candidates.title: Train derailment sends 11 freight cars into wetland in southwest Calgary
candidates.description: Multiple train cars were plunged into a body of water after a train derailed in southwest Calgary on Monday morning. Emergency crews said the train was travelling along the Canadian Pacific Kansas Line when it derailed near Sheriff King Street S.W. at 7:45 a.m.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Agreement reached on multibillion-dollar Pathways carbon capture and storage project
candidates.description: Alberta's government, Ottawa and five major oilsands producers have signed a memorandum of understanding to advance the multibillion-dollar Pathways carbon capture project, a condition for a new West Coast pipeline moving ahead.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Over 800 patients of Brantford, Ont., clinic reminded to get HIV, hepatitis tests after dentist resigns
candidates.description: Grand Erie Public Health is reminding former patients of Dr. George Chan Dentistry in Brantford, Ont., to get tested for hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV. According to files from GEPH obtained by CBC News through a freedom-of-information request, 884 patients were sent letters advising them to get screening. Chan resigned in July, the dental college says.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.S. artist questions local business association's use of AI for merchandise
candidates.description: A local artist says she is surprised that a Truro, N.S., business association used artificial intelligence to design merchandise for a recent community festival.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario runs $8M tab storing U.S. alcohol, as province's plans for stockpile still uncertain
candidates.description: The Ontario government won't explain what will happens to its $79 million U.S. alcohol stockpile, even as new figures obtained by CBC News show taxpayers have spent about $8 million storing it and that roughly $2.6 million worth has expired.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: She's giving her sister a kidney, but didn't know the financial toll
candidates.description: An Ottawa woman who is donating her kidney to her sister says she was shocked to learn that Nova Scotia won't cover travel expenses for her partner to support her.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Pessamit Innu community in Quebec rejects multibillion-dollar hydro deal
candidates.description: Members of the Innu First Nation of Pessamit, northeast of Quebec City, have voted against a $2.5 billion deal that would have allowed Hydro-Québec to carry out energy projects on its ancestral territory.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: With Calgary Stampede wrapped up, Ottawa looks ahead to Alberta referendum in fall
candidates.description: The federal government and those trying to urge Albertans to stay in Canada have a little more than three months to make sure their message sinks in.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.L. teen whose intimate photo was shared says error by RCMP prevented her from getting justice
candidates.description: "It just hurts me, knowing that this could have went through, and that I could have got the justice I wanted," says a teenaged girl in N.L. who said an intimate photo of her was shared multiple times. But when a charge was eventually laid against a youth, the case was dismissed before it got to court.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Former public servant accused in foreign interference case involving China
candidates.description: A former federal scientist in an alleged foreign interference case is accused of copying more than 2,000 documents off a shared departmental server as his lifelong career was nearing its end, with the intention of sharing them with China, CBC News has learned.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Child drowns in southeast Calgary pond
candidates.description: A child drowned in a pond Sunday afternoon, Emergency Health Services (EHS) and the Calgary Fire Department (CFD) say.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. wildfire sparks East Kootenay evacuation alert, weather helps firefight near Boston Bar
candidates.description: Dozens of people have been told to leave their homes on a moment's notice due to an out-of-control wildfire in British Columbia's southeast.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Guinea fowl sales spike in N.S. as homeowners look for natural tick control
candidates.description: Some guinea fowl breeders in Nova Scotia are seeing a surge in demand for the birds to control the tick population. Scientists note they could serve as a supplementary control method, and breeders stress space requirements.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tornado off P.E.I. shore leaves Islanders 'freaking out' with excitement
candidates.description: Laura MacNeil has been running shoreline tours in the Vernon Bridge area of P.E.I. for six years. While her tours typically highlight the Island's fossil sites, MacNeil's tour group Saturday morning got to witness a different kind of natural phenomenon.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Snowbirds fly final hometown show before extended pause
candidates.description: Fifty-five years since their first-ever performance in Moose Jaw, the Snowbirds took to the sky Saturday before what could be an extended pause.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Federal agency touts operation of cybersecurity sensors across the North
candidates.description: The Communications Security Establishment's latest annual report notes that its Cyber Centre deployed the software on government networks in the Northwest Territories in 2022 followed by Yukon and Nunavut in 2024.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.K. counterterrorism police now leading murder probe of ex-MP Ann Widdecombe
candidates.description: After three days in which British authorities suggested there was no information suggesting a political or terroristic motive to last week's killing of longtime MP Ann Widdecombe, it was revealed Monday that the top counterterrorism unit is low leading the investigation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Thai investigators focus on potential negligence in Bangkok club fire that killed 27, injured 73
candidates.description: An explosive fire at a pub in Thailand's capital Bangkok killed 27 people and injured dozens, officials said on Monday, with police investigating possible negligence including obstructed emergency exits as people scrambled ‌to flee the burning venue.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Actor Sam Neill, known for Jurassic Park, dead at 78
candidates.description: Sam Neill, a smoothly elegant and versatile actor whose career moved from art film to blockbuster, has died at the age of 78.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Britney Spears defends her behaviour in moving vehicle 4 months after DUI arrest
candidates.description: Britney Spears said "I think I need to come out of the roof quite a bit more" after being photographed standing with her head through the sunroof of a Mercedes G-wagon as it travelled down a highway near Los Angeles.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Zara's 'death pants' are trending because people keep tripping on them
candidates.description: Technically, the Zara pants at the centre of a new internet phenomenon are called "flowy wide leg pants." But on social media, they're better known as "death trousers" because people keep tripping and falling.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Europe recorded 10,000 excess deaths during late-June heat wave, data shows
candidates.description: European countries reported more than 10,000 excess deaths during the record-breaking heat wave that engulfed the west of the continent in late June, official data shows.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: German hospital treats U.S. Ebola patient infected in Congo
candidates.description: The hospital said there was ‌no risk to the public or other patients, as the individual is being treated in complete isolation in a unit that is ​structurally and organizationally separated from the rest of the facility.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Aging death row inmates set to die in Florida
candidates.description: One of Florida's prisoners scheduled to die in July, a man convicted of killing his ex-girlfriend's parents in 1986, is 80 years old and would be only the second known octogenarian to be executed in the U.S.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham likely died after aorta rupture: medical examiner
candidates.description: Lindsey Graham, 71, was a prominent Republican ​senator from South Carolina. Earlier in ​his career, he was a vocal critic of U.S. President Donald Trump ​before becoming one of his most loyal allies on Capitol Hill.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why the Princess of Wales climbed the highest peaks in England, Scotland and Wales — in a weekend
candidates.description: An outdoor pursuit that took Catherine, Princess of Wales, up peaks in England, Scotland and Wales was both a personal challenge and an opportunity for the House of Windsor to send signals about how a slimmed-down Royal Family approaches its role in trying to be a more relatable 21st-century monarchy.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Sinner beats Zverev to repeat as Wimbledon champion, claim 5th Grand Slam title
candidates.description: Top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev 6-7 (7), 7-6 (2), 6-3, 6-4 Sunday for his second consecutive title at the All England Club after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue following a slip to the grass on a key point in the third set.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuela quakes have killed 4,333, injured 16,740, says National Assembly president
candidates.description: The death toll from the two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24 has risen to 4,333, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez told reporters on Saturday, announcing that the distribution of housing to those affected will begin next week.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada's Dabrowski finishes as Wimbledon women's doubles runner-up for 3rd time
candidates.description: Kristina Mladenovic won a seventh career Grand Slam women's doubles title by teaming up with Guo Hanyu to defeat Canada's Gabriela Dabrowski and Luisa Stefani 6-3, 7-5 in the Wimbledon final Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Home exchanges gaining popularity as Canadians look for cheaper ways to travel
candidates.description: With rising travel costs squeezing vacation budgets, more Canadians are trading keys. Global home exchange networks report a massive surge in Canadian members still looking to travel but save big on accommodations.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Carney talks about Canada's 'energy sovereignty.' But what does that really mean?
candidates.description: Soon after Donald Trump took office for a second time, with punishing tariffs and menacing talk of Canada as "the 51st state," this country's leaders began to invoke the idea of "energy sovereignty." But the Carney government's interpretation of energy sovereignty seems out of step with other countries.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. researchers turn to wild yeast in bid to help struggling craft breweries
candidates.description: Researchers at Langara College and the University of British Columbia are studying wild yeast collected from across B.C. and the Pacific Northwest to see if it can be used to brew beer with unique flavours while reducing costs for breweries.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
candidates.description: It was the first time firefighting planes had been sent up from the normally drier and hotter south of the country to tackle fires in the Paris region.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Infantino opens door to 64-team World Cup
candidates.description: Plans for a 64-team men's world cup are set to be picked up and assessed in detail after the 2026 tournament with Fifa boss Gianni Infantino saying football needs to be "for the whole world".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
candidates.description: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
candidates.description: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Men disguised as police kill Ecuadorean drug lord's brother
candidates.description: The victim, David Macías, was the brother of drug lord 'El Fito', who is awaiting trial in the US.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US Senator Mitch McConnell says absence due to fall and pneumonia
candidates.description: The veteran Republican says he will not return to work "quite yet", in his first statement following weeks of speculation about his health.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: A versatile actor whose roles went far beyond Jurassic Park - Sam Neill dies aged 78
candidates.description: The actor, who has died aged 78, built up a long string of screen credits over a five-decade career.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The SpaceX IPO made history. One month on has it lost momentum?
candidates.description: A month on from the firm's stock market debut, the reality of how SpaceX currently makes money has seemed to come into clearer focus.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all
candidates.description: Michael Steranka, vice president at the mobile game's owner Scopely, says it has always been about bringing people together.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bombs, blood, ambushes - football coaching with the army
candidates.description: From kidnappings to invasions, military training takes football coaches out of their comfort zone as part of the Football Association of Wales' Uefa pro licence course.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: People flee Bangkok bar as huge flames shoot out from doorway
candidates.description: At least 28 people have died and 25 are critically injured after a fire tore through a bar in a popular Bangkok district.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: At the scene where Bangkok's deadly bar fire unfolded
candidates.description: The BBC’s South East Asia correspondent visits the site where at least 27 people were killed during a bar fire in Thailand.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
candidates.description: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump says the U.S. will reinstate its blockade on Iran and charge a toll on ships
candidates.description: The move comes after the U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, raising the specter of a return to all-out war after a shaky ceasefire took effect in June.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
candidates.description: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
candidates.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Investigators probe a fire at a music bar in Bangkok that killed at least 27 people
candidates.description: By Monday morning, forensic teams were investigating the scene. Officials say many victims were trapped in windowless bathrooms near a rear exit. Investigators are focusing on the ceiling above the performance stage for clues.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
candidates.description: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
candidates.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran and Tehran attacks U.S. allies in the Gulf
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
candidates.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
candidates.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: States sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros blockbuster merger
candidates.description: California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: EV battery recycling has a math problem
candidates.description: For some electric vehicle batteries, it costs more to recycle them than they're worth. That creates all kinds of problems, which a new Colorado law aims to solve.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
candidates.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pythons' extreme biology may hold clues for treating human disease
candidates.description: These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
candidates.description: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
candidates.description: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Influencers are questioning common guidance about breast milk. Here's what science says
candidates.description: The guidance over leftover breast milk says toss it after 2 hours. But a recent preprint study shared on social media has some people questioning that advice.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A promising tale from Senegal of fish, rice .... and dangerous worms
candidates.description: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Flood Watch issued July 13 at 11:11AM PDT until July 15 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Elko NV
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flash flooding caused by excessive rainfall is possible. * WHERE...Portions of eastern, north central, northeast, and south central Nevada, including the following areas, in eastern Nevada, White Pine. In north central Nevada, Northern Lander and Northern Eureka and Southern Lander and Southern Eureka. In northeast Nevada, Northern Elko, Ruby Mountains and East Humboldt Range in Elko, South Central Elko, Southeastern Elko and Southwest Elko. In south central Nevada, Northeastern Nye and Northwestern Nye. * WHEN...From Wednesday morning through Wednesday evening. * IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying and flood-prone locations. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - Deep subtropical moisture will be moving northward across portions of northern and central Nevada through tomorrow. This anonymously moist atmosphere will allow for the development of showers and thunderstorms. Some of these storms will be capable of producing rainfall rates of an inch of rainfall per hour or more, leading to flash flooding. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 13 at 2:09PM EDT until July 13 at 4:15PM EDT by NWS Columbia SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...East Central Calhoun County in central South Carolina... West Central Clarendon County in central South Carolina... Southwestern Kershaw County in central South Carolina... Eastern Richland County in central South Carolina... Southwestern Sumter County in central South Carolina... * WHEN...Until 415 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 209 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Columbia, Forest Acres, McEntire Air Base, Dentsville, Elgin, Arcadia Lakes, Eastover, Horrel Hill, Gadsden, Fort Jackson, Sesquicentennial State Park, Villages at Sandhill, Woodfield, Poinsett State Park, Lake Carolina, Killian, Blythewood, Pinewood and Paxville. - This includes the following highways... Interstate 20 in South Carolina between mile markers 73 and 89. Interstate 77 between mile markers 13 and 28. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued July 13 at 2:05PM EDT until July 13 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL
candidates.description: SMWTAE The National Weather Service in Tallahassee has issued a * Special Marine Warning for... Apalachee Bay or Coastal Waters From Keaton Beach to Ochlockonee River Fl out to 20 Nm... Coastal Waters From Ochlockonee River to Apalachicola FL out to 20 Nm... Coastal Waters from Mexico Beach to Apalachicola out 20 NM... Waters from Mexico Beach to Apalachicola FL from 20 to 60 NM... Waters from Suwannee River to Apalachicola FL from 20 to 60 NM... * Until 500 PM EDT/400 PM CDT/. * At 205 PM EDT/105 PM CDT/, strong thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 22 nm southwest of Panama City Beach to 47 nm southwest of Empire Mica Wreck, moving east at 30 knots. HAZARD...Wind gusts to 40 knots. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect wind gusts in excess of 34 knots...suddenly higher waves...frequent lightning...and heavy downpours. Make sure all on board are wearing life jackets. * Locations impacted include... O Tower, K Tower, S Tower, V Tower, Apalachicola, C Tower, and St George Island.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 13 at 1:04PM CDT until July 13 at 3:00PM CDT by NWS Houston/Galveston TX
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...A portion of southeast Texas, including the following counties, Brazoria, Chambers and Galveston. * WHEN...Until 300 PM CDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 104 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued July 13 at 1:04PM CDT until July 13 at 2:15PM CDT by NWS Mobile AL
candidates.description: SMWMOB The National Weather Service in Mobile has issued a * Special Marine Warning for... Waters from Pensacola FL to Pascagoula MS from 20 to 60 NM... * Until 215 PM CDT. * At 103 PM CDT, strong thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 33 nm southwest of Farewell Buoy to 43 nm south of Sand Island Lighthouse to 40 nm east of Pilottown, moving east at 30 knots. HAZARD...Wind gusts 34 knots or greater. SOURCE...Radar. IMPACT...Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. * Strong thunderstorms will remain over mainly open waters.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Marine Weather Statement issued July 13 at 2:02PM EDT by NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL
candidates.description: A Marine Weather Statement has been issued for the following areas... Coastal waters from Tarpon Springs to Suwannee River FL out 20 NM... Waters from Tarpon Springs to Suwannee River FL out 20 to 60 NM... At 202 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated strong thunderstorms, capable of producing winds to around 30 knots. These thunderstorms were located along a line extending from near Yankeetown to 20 nm southwest of Cedar Key to 51 nm south of V Tower, moving southeast at 25 knots.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 13 at 12:58PM CDT until July 13 at 3:00PM CDT by NWS Houston/Galveston TX
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...A portion of southeast Texas, including the following counties, Grimes, Harris, Montgomery and Waller. * WHEN...Until 300 PM CDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. Ponding of water in urban or other areas is occurring or is imminent. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 1258 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2.5 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 13 at 12:57PM CDT until July 22 at 7:00AM CDT by NWS Jackson MS
candidates.description: ...The Flood Advisory is extended for the following rivers in Louisiana...Mississippi... Mississippi River At Natchez affecting Adams, Wilkinson and Concordia Counties. * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall continues. * WHERE...Mississippi River at Natchez. * WHEN...Until Wednesday, July 22. * IMPACTS...At 38.0 feet, Lowlands along the river in areas not protected by levees begin to flood. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 12:00 PM CDT Monday the stage was 37.6 feet. - Forecast...The river is expected to fluctuate over the next week, before rising to near 38.0 feet again Tuesday, July 21. - Action stage is 38.0 feet. - Flood stage is 48.0 feet. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 13 at 12:55PM CDT until July 13 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: * WHAT...or the Dense Fog Advisory, dense fog will reduce visibility to less than 1 NM. For the Small Craft Advisory, southwest winds around 15 kt with gusts up to 25 kt and waves 1 to 2 ft expected. * WHERE...Grand Marais to Taconite Harbor MN, Taconite Harbor to Silver Bay Harbor MN and Silver Bay Harbor to Two Harbors MN. * WHEN...For the Dense Fog Advisory, until 7 PM CDT this evening. For the Small Craft Advisory, until 9 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft. Low visibility will make navigation difficult.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued July 13 at 12:55PM CDT until July 14 at 6:00AM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: * WHAT...Southwest winds 10 to 15 kt with gusts up to 25 kt and waves 1 to 3 ft expected. * WHERE...Outer Apostle Islands Beyond 5 NM from Mainland. * WHEN...Until 6 AM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: M 4.3 - 18 km WSW of Johannesburg, CA
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 4.31762677888687. The event occurred near 18 km WSW of Johannesburg, CA. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey

candidates.title: M 6.3 - southeast of the Loyalty Islands
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 6.3. The event occurred near southeast of the Loyalty Islands. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey

candidates.title: M 6.4 - 191 km SE of Lorengau, Papua New Guinea
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 6.4. The event occurred near 191 km SE of Lorengau, Papua New Guinea. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey

candidates.title: M 4.1 - 2 km ESE of Frazier Park, CA
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 4.10922888670317. The event occurred near 2 km ESE of Frazier Park, CA. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
